steps.question: What have you been dealing with?
What have you tried so far?
How long has this been going on?
steps.freeText: a dry fast of 6 days like 7 years ago

steps.question: Where are things at right now?
Do you feel like you can figure this out on your own?

report:
Five-plus years of brain fog and digestive issues, and you've already been through the cycle: supplements, dietary changes, doctors, even functional medicine practitioners. The fact that you're still stuck tells you something important. These two problems aren't separate. When the gut is compromised, it generates inflammation and bacterial byproducts that reach the brain directly, creating that fog you can't think your way out of. And when your body's energy systems are suppressed by that same gut dysfunction, your digestion slows further, bacteria build up, and the whole loop tightens. That six-day dry fast seven years ago was likely a significant hit to your gut lining and microbial balance, and everything since has been your body trying to compensate without the foundation it needs. The up-and-down pattern you're experiencing is textbook for this kind of self-reinforcing cycle: you intervene on one piece, feel a little better, then the underlying web pulls things back. After five years, this pattern doesn't resolve on its own. It deepens.

You've clearly done the work of trying. The issue isn't effort or willingness. It's that when gut dysfunction, impaired energy metabolism, stress physiology, and neuroinflammation are all feeding each other, addressing them one at a time will never hold. Every supplement you've tried, every protocol a practitioner gave you, was likely targeting a real piece of the problem. But without a coordinated strategy that hits these systems together, in the right sequence, with adjustments based on how your body actually responds, you keep landing back where you started. You said solving this alone might be difficult, and honestly, that instinct is right. This is exactly the kind of interconnected problem that requires a team who understands how these systems talk to each other and can move them in concert.

The next page will show you exactly how we do that.
